learningGoals: Identify and name 3 common wetland plants in English.
Observe and record plant buoyancy in a field journal.
Practice using the sentence structure 'I see a [color] [plant].'
lessons.activitySummary: Students will go on a 'Plant Scavenger Hunt' along the East Lake shore to find floating and rooted plants. They will conduct a simple buoyancy experiment by placing different natural items in the water to see what floats.
lessons.safetyNotes: Keep a 2-meter distance from the water's edge; wear life vests if approaching docks.

learningGoals: Use binoculars to spot and count 3 different bird species.
Understand why birds need wetlands for food and shelter.
Use basic adjectives to describe bird movements (fast, slow, high, low).
lessons.activitySummary: Students will use binoculars to observe migratory birds and map out where they find 'homes' in the park. They will then sketch a bird nest using twigs and dried grass found on the ground.
lessons.safetyNotes: Avoid feeding the birds; stay on the designated wooden boardwalks to protect habitats.

learningGoals: Discover and identify 3 types of wetland insects (e.g., dragonfly).
Compare the number of legs and wings on different bugs.
Learn the 'Head, Thorax, Abdomen' song in English.
lessons.activitySummary: Using magnifying glasses, students will explore the tall grass areas to find insects. They will act out the life cycle of a dragonfly from water-dweller to flyer through a movement-based game.
lessons.safetyNotes: Wear long pants to prevent bug bites; do not touch unknown insects; apply sunscreen.

learningGoals: Differentiate between dry sand and wet mud by touch and sight.
Build a simple 'mini-dam' to see how wetlands slow down water.
Use the verbs 'build', 'pour', and 'stop' in context.
lessons.activitySummary: Students will participate in a 'Mud Kitchen' where they test how water moves through different soil types. They will work in teams to build a small barrier using rocks and mud to protect a toy animal's home.
lessons.safetyNotes: Ensure all students wash hands thoroughly after touching soil; stay within the marked 'Discovery Zone'.
